}

    // Check if the requested bytes_to_read is within the available data in the buffer_adaptor
    if (bytes_to_read > (adaptor.write_ - adaptor.read_)) {
        throw std::runtime_error("Requested read size exceeds available data");
    }
    
    // Create a destination vector and use the read function to read the data
    std::vector<uint8_t> destination(bytes_to_read);
    adaptor.read(destination.data(), bytes_to_read);
    
    return destination;
}

void write_buffer_data(std::vector<uint8_t>& dest_buffer, const std::vector<uint8_t>& source_data) {
    // Create a buffer_adaptor object from the dest_buffer
    hexi::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<uint8_t>> adaptor(dest_buffer);
    
    // Check if there is enough space in the buffer_adaptor to write the source_data
    if (source_data.size() > (adaptor.buffer_.size() - adaptor.write_)) {
        throw std::runtime_error("Insufficient space in buffer for write operation");
    }
    
    // Use the write function to write the source_data to the buffer_adaptor
    adaptor.write(source_data.data(), source_data.size());
}

```cpp
#include <hexi/endian.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>
#include <string>
#include <string_view>

struct MeasurementData {
    uint32_t sensor_id;
    std::vector<float> temperatures;
    double pressure;
    
    template<typename Adaptor>
    void serialise(Adaptor& adaptor) {
        if constexpr (adaptor.is_reading) {
            // Deserialization
            adaptor >> sensor_id;
            for (auto& temp : temperatures) {
                float t;
                adaptor >> t;
                hexi::endian::little_to_native_inplace(t);
                temp = t;
            }
            adaptor >> pressure;
            hexi::endian::little_to_native_inplace(pressure);
        } else {
            // Serialization
            adaptor << sensor_id;
            for (const auto& temp : temperatures) {
                auto converted = hexi::endian::conditional_reverse(temp, std::endian::native, std::endian::little);
                adaptor << converted;
            }
            auto converted_pressure = hexi::endian::conditional_reverse(pressure, std::endian::native, std::endian::little);
            adaptor << converted_pressure;
        }
    }
};

std::vector<uint8_t> serialize_measurement_data(const MeasurementData& data) {
    hexi::Buffer buffer;
    hexi::binary_stream stream(buffer);
    stream.serialise(data);
    return buffer.to_vector(); // Assuming Buffer has a method to convert to vector
}

MeasurementData deserialize_measurement_data(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    hexi::Buffer buf;
    buf.write(buffer.data(), buffer.size());
    hexi::binary_stream stream(buf);
    MeasurementData data;
    stream.deserialise(data);
    return data;
}

struct SensorInfo {
    uint32_t sensor_id;
    std::string_view sensor_name;
};

SensorInfo extract_sensor_info(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    hexi::Buffer buf;
    buf.write(buffer.data(), buffer.size());
    hexi::binary_stream stream(buf);
    
    // Extract sensor name (null-terminated string)
    auto sensor_name = stream.view();
    
    // Check if there's enough data for sensor_id using enforce_read_bounds
    // But enforce_read_bounds is private, so we cannot call it.
    // Instead, we try to read the sensor_id and check the stream state.
    // If the stream state is not ok, then we return a default SensorInfo.
    
    // Read sensor_id
    uint32_t sensor_id;
    stream >> sensor_id;
    
    if (!stream.good()) {
        // Not enough data, return default
        return {0, ""};
    }
    
    // Skip the remaining data
    stream.skip(buffer.size() - (sensor_name.size() + sizeof(sensor_id)));
    
    return {sensor_id, sensor_name};
}
```

This code is based on the assumptions and the reference materials. There are several points where the reference materials are incomplete, so I had to make assumptions.

I hope this meets the requirements.
